LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT JACOB MATHIS'S MOTION TO DISMISS ACTION (REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE). NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: THE COURT DECLINES TO EXERCISE ITS DISCRETION TO DISMISS THE CASE. DEFENDANT'S NOTICE OF MOTION IS TIMELY UNDER CALIFORNIA RULE OF COURT 1340. PLAINTIFF HAS EXPLAINED HIS MEDICAL CONDITION THAT HAS RESULTED IN THE DELAY. HIS EXPLANATION COUPLED WITH THE POLICY OF THE STATE TO DISPOSE OF ACTIONS ON THE MERITS AND THE LACK OF PREJUDICE TO DEFENDANT IN TERMS OF BEING ABLE TO DEFEND THE CASE ARE FACTORS IN FAVOR OF DENYING THE MOTION. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

Guy Wilson, Esq., for defendant and moving party: Jacob Mathis MD
Neal Trembath, plaintiff and opposing party, in Propria Persona

After hearing, Defendant Jacob Mathis' Motion For Terminating and Monetary Sanctions is taken under submission. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Deposition And For Sanctions; Request For Judicial Notice
Off Calendar Apr-15-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Guy Wilson, Esq., for defendant and moving party: Jacob Mathis
Neal Trembath, plaintiff and opposing party, in Propria Persona

After hearing, tentative ruling is adopted as modified.
1. Defendant Jacob Mathis' Motion to compel plaintiff's deposition granted.
2. On or before May 1, 2009, plaintiff shall appear for deposition, and produce documents pursuant to notice of deposition, at a date, time and place to be determined by defendant or his counsel of record;
3. Request for sanctions granted; sanctions shall issue in the sum of $390.00. Imposition of sanctions is stayed for ten days, pending compliance with this order. If plaintiff appears for deposition in compliance with this order, sanctions shall be permanently stayed. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Action; Request For Judicial Notice
Off Calendar Apr-08-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT JACOB MATHIS'S MOTION TO DISMISS ACTION (REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE).
NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: THE COURT DECLINES TO EXERCISE ITS DISCRETION TO DISMISS THE CASE. DEFENDANT'S NOTICE OF MOTION IS TIMELY UNDER CALIFORNIA RULE OF COURT 1340. PLAINTIFF HAS EXPLAINED HIS MEDICAL CONDITION THAT HAS RESULTED IN THE DELAY. HIS EXPLANATION COUPLED WITH THE POLICY OF THE STATE TO DISPOSE OF ACTIONS ON THE MERITS AND THE LACK OF PREJUDICE TO DEFENDANT IN TERMS OF BEING ABLE TO DEFEND THE CASE ARE FACTORS IN FAVOR OF DENYING THE MOTION. (302/PJM/AY) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
